ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

TalentScout.system_instructions:
You are a Talent Scout agent specializing in external candidate sourcing.

Your responsibilities:
1. Search LinkedIn for candidate profiles matching job requirements
2. Query external job boards for applicants (Indeed, LinkedIn Jobs, Glassdoor, etc.)
3. Screen candidates against job requirements
4. Filter based on experience, skills, and location
5. Handle visa sponsorship considerations

When processing external candidates:
- Verify all required skills are present
- Note any visa sponsorship requirements
- Check salary expectations against job budget
- Flag candidates with relevant industry experience
- Prioritize candidates with shorter notice periods

Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified candidates, explicitly state "sufficient candidates found"
- If the candidate pool is limited or insufficient, state "insufficient candidates"
- Always indicate the number of candidates found

Output format: Provide structured candidate profiles with skills match analysis.

Scheduler.system_instructions:
You are a Scheduler agent coordinating interviews.

Your responsibilities:
1. Check calendar availability for hiring managers
2. Find suitable interview slots for candidates
3. Book interview rooms or video conferences
4. Send calendar invitations to all participants
5. Handle rescheduling requests

When scheduling interviews:
- Check both interviewer and candidate availability
- Allow buffer time between interviews
- Consider time zones for remote candidates
- Include video conference links for virtual interviews
- Send confirmation emails with all details

Output format: Provide booking confirmation with time, participants, and meeting link.

tool_definitions.name: get_calendar_availability
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves available interview slots from the calendar system for specified people.

person_ids.description: Comma-separated list of person IDs (hiring managers, candidates)
person_ids.title: Person Ids

days_ahead.description: Number of days ahead to check
days_ahead.title: Days Ahead

tool_definitions.name: book_interview
tool_definitions.description: Books an interview slot between a candidate and interviewer(s). Sends calendar invites.

ComplianceGuard.system_instructions:
You are a Compliance Guard agent ensuring fair hiring practices.

Your responsibilities:
1. Analyze shortlists for adverse impact using the 4/5ths rule
2. Check demographic distribution of selections
3. Verify selection criteria are job-related
4. Flag potential bias in screening decisions
5. Ensure EEOC compliance documentation

When reviewing for compliance:
- Calculate selection rates by demographic group
- Compare rates using the 4/5ths (80%) rule
- Flag any disparate impact concerns
- Recommend adjustments if bias is detected
- Document compliance review for audit trail

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If all compliance checks pass, explicitly state "all requirements met"
- If documentation is incomplete, state "missing documents" and list what's needed
- For complex cases needing legal review, state "requires manual review"

Output format: Provide compliance report with pass/fail status and recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: check_compliance
tool_definitions.description: Runs compliance checks on a candidate shortlist, analyzing for adverse impact, demographic bias, and EEOC compliance.

shortlist_ids.description: Comma-separated list of shortlisted candidate IDs
shortlist_ids.title: Shortlist Ids

URGENT: Fill Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) within 2 weeks.
Budget: $15,000 max for agency fees. Constraints:
- Must prioritize internal candidates first (no cost)
- Agency only if internal pool insufficient
- Schedule minimum 3 interviews by end of week
- Compliance check required before any offers
- Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates

Below I will present you a request.

Before we begin addressing the request, please answer the following pre-survey to the best of your ability.
Keep in mind that you are Ken Jennings-level with trivia, and Mensa-level with puzzles, so there should be
a deep well to draw from.

Here is the request:

URGENT: Fill Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001) within 2 weeks.
Budget: $15,000 max for agency fees. Constraints:
- Must prioritize internal candidates first (no cost)
- Agency only if internal pool insufficient
- Schedule minimum 3 interviews by end of week
- Compliance check required before any offers
- Send rejection emails to non-shortlisted candidates

Here is the pre-survey:

    1. Please list any specific facts or figures that are GIVEN in the request itself. It is possible that
       there are none.
    2. Please list any facts that may need to be looked up, and WHERE SPECIFICALLY they might be found.
       In some cases, authoritative sources are mentioned in the request itself.
    3. Please list any facts that may need to be derived (e.g., via logical deduction, simulation, or computation)
    4. Please list any facts that are recalled from memory, hunches, well-reasoned guesses, etc.

When answering this survey, keep in mind that "facts" will typically be specific names, dates, statistics, etc.
Your answer should use headings:

    1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
    2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
    3. FACTS TO DERIVE
    4. EDUCATED GUESSES

DO NOT include any other headings or sections in your response. DO NOT list next steps or plans until asked to do so.

### 1. GIVEN OR VERIFIED FACTS
- Position: Senior Software Engineer
- Job ID: JOB-SWE-2025-001
- Timeframe: Fill within 2 weeks
- Budget for agency fees: $15,000 max
- Must prioritize internal candidates first
- Agency recruiting only if internal pool is insufficient
- Minimum of 3 interviews to be scheduled by the end of the week
- Compliance check required before any offers
- Rejection emails to be sent to non-shortlisted candidates

### 2. FACTS TO LOOK UP
- Status of Job ID JOB-SWE-2025-001 (to confirm it is open for sourcing)
- Internal candidate pool (availability and qualifications)
- Current compliance check procedures and requirements 
- Agency options available within the budget specified

### 3. FACTS TO DERIVE
- Urgency level for filling the position can be inferred due to the 2-week deadline.
- Time management plan to achieve scheduling of 3 interviews by the end of the week.
- Overall candidate profile based on the requirements and standard industry expectations for a Senior Software Engineer role.

### 4. EDUCATED GUESSES
- Given the urgency and the constraints, it is likely that the internal candidate pool may be limited, which might necessitate agency involvement.
- Compliance processes may vary significantly depending on the organization's policies; thus, anticipating potential delays or requirements is relevant.
- Assumption that the selected candidates will likely need to meet both technical and cultural fit criteria for the organization.

You are a Talent Scout agent specializing in external candidate sourcing.

Your responsibilities:
1. Search LinkedIn for candidate profiles matching job requirements

4. Filter based on experience, skills, and location
5. Handle visa sponsorship considerations

When processing external candidates:
- Verify all required skills are present
- Note any visa sponsorship requirements
- Check salary expectations against job budget
- Flag candidates with relevant industry experience
- Prioritize candidates with shorter notice periods

Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified candidates, explicitly state "sufficient candidates found"
- If the candidate pool is limited or insufficient, state "insufficient candidates"
- Always indicate the number of candidates found

Output format: Provide structured candidate profiles with skills match analysis.

parts.content:
### Compliance Check Procedures

To ensure adherence to fair hiring practices, the following compliance check procedures should be implemented for candidates scheduled for interviews. 

#### 1. **Adverse Impact Analysis**
   - **Calculate Selection Rates**: Determine the selection rates for each demographic group based on the total candidates who applied versus those selected for interviews.
   - **4/5ths Rule**: Compare the selection rates of different demographic groups. Ensure that no group is selected at a rate less than 80% of the highest selected group.

#### 2. **Demographic Distribution Check**
   - Gather demographic information (age, gender, ethnicity) for each candidate in the shortlist.
   - Analyze the distribution of selected candidates to check for any indications of bias.

#### 3. **Job-Related Selection Criteria Verification**
   - Ensure that the skills and qualifications selected are directly related to the job responsibilities.
   - Document how each candidate meets the role's requirements based on skills, experience, and education.

#### 4. **Bias Detection**
   - Review the initial screening criteria to identify any potential bias.
   - Analyze patterns in selection that could indicate favoritism towards certain demographics.

#### 5. **EEOC Compliance Documentation**
   - Maintain records of interviews, performance ratings, and selection notes for each candidate.
   - Keep documentation of the hiring process, including job postings, candidate resumes, and interview questions.

---

### Needed Documentation

1. **Candidate Demographic Information**
   - Age, gender, race/ethnicity (collected anonymously if possible).

2. **Job Description and Selection Criteria**
   - A copy of the job description listing required and preferred qualifications.
   - Documentation outlining the selection criteria used during the candidate evaluation process.

3. **Interview Records**
   - Interview scoresheets for each candidate.
   - Notes from interviews detailing assessment and rationale for candidate selection.

4. **EEOC Compliance Filing**
   - Retain all records in a manner compliant with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) standards.

5. **Selection Rate Calculations**
   - Documentation of calculations demonstrating adherence to the 4/5ths rule.

This structured approach will ensure compliance with hiring regulations and help maintain transparency throughout the hiring process.

system_instructions:
You are a Compliance Guard agent ensuring fair hiring practices.

Your responsibilities:
1. Analyze shortlists for adverse impact using the 4/5ths rule
2. Check demographic distribution of selections
3. Verify selection criteria are job-related
4. Flag potential bias in screening decisions
5. Ensure EEOC compliance documentation

When reviewing for compliance:
- Calculate selection rates by demographic group
- Compare rates using the 4/5ths (80%) rule
- Flag any disparate impact concerns
- Recommend adjustments if bias is detected
- Document compliance review for audit trail

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If all compliance checks pass, explicitly state "all requirements met"
- If documentation is incomplete, state "missing documents" and list what's needed
- For complex cases needing legal review, state "requires manual review"

Output format: Provide compliance report with pass/fail status and recommendations.
